reasoning_model_1: Model 1 fails significantly in Content Accuracy. The required script was simply "This lecture is so boring, it makes me yawn." However, the model hallucinates and adds a substantial amount of text: "Yeah, I'm totally yawning right now, can't even keep my eyes open." Regarding the core paralinguistic requirement (Non-Linguistic Vocalizations), the model does not produce the sound of a yawn. Instead, it merely speaks about yawning in a slightly monotonous tone. There is no audible non-linguistic vocalization of a yawn at any point (0:00-0:07). Because the content is clearly inconsistent with the script due to major additions and the target feature is absent, this receives a score of 0.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 demonstrates excellent performance. In terms of Content Accuracy, it adheres strictly to the script: "This lecture is so boring, it makes me yawn." regarding the text spoken. In terms of Paralinguistic Compliance (Non-Linguistic Vocalizations), the audio begins with a very distinct, natural-sounding yawn (0:00-0:02) before the speech begins. The subsequent speech is delivered with a weary, bored tone that matches the context of the yawn perfectly. The integration of the vocalization is natural and meets the user's command precisely.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 fails on two major counts: it hallucinates extra sentences not present in the prompt (violating content accuracy), and it fails to actually generate the required sound effect (a yawn), instead just talking about yawning. Model 2, conversely, follows the script perfectly and includes a high-quality, realistic non-linguistic vocalization (a yawn) at the beginning of the clip (0:00-0:02), fulfilling the prompt's core requirement. T1 receives a 0 due to content hallucination and missing features.
